content.claim: In Hungary, school restructuring has occurred via church takeover rather than outright closure. Church-run schools in ethnically mixed rural areas with limited exit options have accelerated ethnic and social sorting: segregated schools operated by religious organizations increased from <1% (2010) to 14% (2019), while ethnically mixed primary schools decreased from 14.7% to 12.6%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Data measure operator type change and segregation indices but do not isolate the causal effect of school closures per se from other institutional restructuring
Does not specify whether these segregation patterns reflect family choice or selection bias by school operators
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
